qa_pairs.question: How can a Principal Investigator (PI) add users to their resource allocation for general-purpose Alliance clusters or Niagara?
qa_pairs.answer: To add users to an allocation, a PI must first log in to https://ccdb.alliancecan.ca/. From the 'My Account' menu, they should select 'Manage RAP Memberships', which leads to https://ccdb.alliancecan.ca/resource_allocation_projects/members. On this page, they select the desired Resource Allocation Project (RAP) from the drop-down list. To add a new member, they can go to 'Add Members' and enter the user's CCRI, or use the 'In bulk' link to add multiple sponsored users or co-PIs associated with another PI simultaneously.

qa_pairs.question: When submitting jobs to a general-purpose Alliance cluster, how should users specify which allocation their job pertains to?
qa_pairs.answer: When submitting jobs to the scheduler on a general-purpose Alliance cluster, users need to specify a group name as the value of the `--account` option. Jobs related to the research described in a Resource Allocation Competition (RAC) application should be submitted with the group name corresponding to the RAC award, for example, `--account=rrg-profname-ab`. For jobs pertaining to other research, the default group name should be used, such as `--account=def-profname`.

qa_pairs.question: What is the structure and purpose of a `/project` storage allocation on a general-purpose Alliance cluster?
qa_pairs.answer: On a general-purpose Alliance cluster, a `/project` storage allocation is created as a directory with the format `/project/<group-name>`, for instance, `/project/rrg-profname-ab`. This directory is associated with a quota defining the amount of data that can be stored. All sponsored users should store files pertaining to the research described in the Resource Allocation Competition (RAC) application within this dedicated `/project` directory.
